title: ParseReply
text: ParseReply Parses a line from a single-line or multi-line response from an SMTP server into the reply code and the associated message. int ParseReply(unsigned char * Reply , int ReplyLen , int * Code , int * Offset , int * Last , unsigned char ** Next ) Parameters Type Name Direction Description unsigned char * Reply IN Reply int * ReplyLen IN Length of Reply int * Code OUT Reply code int * Offset OUT Offset from start of reply to the textual message int * Last OUT Flag set to indicate last or only line unsigned char ** Next OUT Pointer to next line. The caller should not change * Next between calls. When starting with a reply, * Next must be 0. Return Values Value Description >= 0 Length of the message text without the CRLF termination. There are no more lines in the reply when Last is non-zero. -1 Internal call error; syntax or call is not understood. -2 Line does not start with a digit. -3 Line does not end with CRLF. Description This function parses a line from a single-line or multi-line response from an SMTP server into the reply code and the associated message. The function can be used to walk through the lines of a multi-line response. The line format is: MessNum {sp | - } MessText CRLF MessNum 3-digit number sp (space) Iindicates that it is a single line reply or the last line of reply - (hyphen) Indicates that it is a multi-line response MessText Associated message text CRLF Carriage return and line feed characters For example: Explain this code 123-First line 123-Second line 123-234 Text beginning with numbers 123 Last line len = ParseReply(sec->InBuf, sec->InDataLen, &code, &offset, &last, &next); Copy Was this topic helpful?
